title: Systems and methods for phase-modulated radar detection
abstract: A system for phase-modulated radar detection, preferably including one or more transmitter arrays, receiver arrays, and signal processors. A method for phase-modulated radar detection, preferably including transmitting a set of probe signals, receiving a set of reflected probe signals, and/or decoding the set of received probe signals, and optionally including evaluating effects of phase variance and/or modifying probe signal characteristics.
claims:
Claims We claim: l. A method for radar detection, comprising: â¢ at a set of transmit elements of a radar array, transmitting a first plurality of multiplexed radar probe signals, comprising: â¢ at a first transmit element of the set, based on a first encoding, transmitting a first encoded set of radar probe signals; and â¢ concurrent with transmitting the first encoded set, at a second transmit element of the set, based on a second encoding different from the first encoding, transmitting a second encoded set of radar probe signals, wherein the radar array defines a first displacement vector from the first transmit element to the second transmit element; â¢ at the set of transmit elements, after transmitting the first plurality, transmitting a second plurality of multiplexed radar probe signals phase shifted from the first plurality based on a first set of phase variances, wherein transmitting the second plurality comprises: â¢ at the first transmit element, based on the first encoding, transmitting a third encoded set of radar probe signals; and â¢ concurrent with transmitting the third encoded set, at the second transmit element, based on the second encoding, transmitting a fourth encoded set of radar probe signals; â¢ at a set of receive elements of the radar array, receiving a plurality of reflected signals comprising a first set of reflected signals and a second set of reflected signals, wherein receiving the plurality of reflected signals comprises: â¢ in response to generation of the first set of reflected signals via reflection of the first and second encoded sets by a set of targets in an environment surrounding the radar array, receiving the first set of reflected signals; and 

 â¢ in response to generation of the second set of reflected signals via reflection of the third and fourth encoded sets by the set of targets, receiving the second set of reflected signals; â¢ based on the first and second encodings, demultiplexing the first set of reflected signals to generate a first decoded set of signals associated with the first transmit element and a second decoded set of signals associated with the second transmit element; â¢ based on the first decoded set, determining target information associated with a first physical receive element of the set of receive elements; â¢ based on the second decoded set, determining target information associated with a virtual receive element location displaced from the first physical receive element by the first displacement vector; â¢ based on the first and second encodings, demultiplexing the second set of reflected signals to generate a third decoded set of signals associated with the first transmit element and a fourth decoded set of signals associated with the second transmit element; and â¢ based on the first, second, third, and fourth decoded sets, determining target information associated with an interpolated virtual receive element location arranged between the first physical receive element and the virtual receive element location. 2. The method of Claim l, wherein the first set of phase variances are associated with a first beam steering angle for the set of transmit elements. 3. The method of Claim 1, wherein each phase variance of the first set has a respective magnitude less than or equal to ioÂ°. 4. The method of Claim 1, wherein: â¢ the set of transmit elements further comprises a third transmit element; â¢ transmitting the second plurality of multiplexed radar probe signals further comprises, concurrent with transmitting the third and fourth encoded sets, at the third transmit element, transmitting a fifth encoded set of radar probe signals; and 

 â¢ the first set of phase variances are associated with a 2-dimensional phase space. 5. The method of Claim 4, wherein: â¢ the radar array defines a second displacement vector from the first transmit element to the third transmit element; and â¢ the first and second displacement vectors are substantially non-parallel. 6. The method of Claim 5, wherein the first set of phase variances are associated with a first beam steering azimuthal angle and a first beam steering elevation angle for the set of transmit elements. 7. The method of Claim 5, wherein the first and second displacement vectors are substantially perpendicular. 8. The method of Claim 1, further comprising, based on the target information associated with the first physical receive element, the virtual receive element location, and the interpolated virtual receive element location, determining a looking angle to a target of the set of targets. 9. The method of Claim 1, wherein: â¢ the first and second pluralities of multiplexed radar probe signals define a radar wavelength; and â¢ a length of the displacement vector is substantially greater than half the radar wavelength. 10. The method of Claim 9, wherein a distance between the first physical receive element and the interpolated virtual receive element location is substantially equal to half the radar wavelength. 11. The method of Claim 1, wherein the first encoding is substantially orthogonal to the second encoding. 12. The method of Claim 11, wherein the first and second encodings are binary phase modulation encodings. 13. The method of Claim 1, wherein: â¢ the method further comprises, at the set of transmit elements, after transmitting the second plurality, transmitting a third plurality of multiplexed radar probe 

 signals phase shifted from the first plurality based on a second set of phase variances different from the first set of phase variances; â¢ receiving the plurality of reflected signals further comprises, in response to generation of a third set of reflected signals via reflection of the third plurality of multiplexed radar probe signals by the set of targets, receiving the third set of reflected signals; â¢ the method further comprises, based on the first and second encodings, demultiplexing the third set of reflected signals to generate a fifth decoded set of signals associated with the first transmit element and a sixth decoded set of signals associated with the second transmit element; and â¢ determining target information associated with the interpolated virtual receive element location is performed based further on the fifth and sixth decoded sets. 14. The method of Claim 13, further comprising: â¢ determining that a Doppler variance of the plurality of reflected signals is less than a threshold value; â¢ in response to determining that the Doppler variance is less than the threshold value: â¢ determining a third set of phase variances, different from the first and second sets of phase variances; and â¢ at the set of transmit elements, transmitting a transmitting a fourth plurality of multiplexed radar probe signals phase shifted from the first plurality based on the third set of phase variances; â¢ at the set of receive elements, in response to generation of a fourth set of reflected signals via reflection of the fourth plurality of multiplexed radar probe signals by the set of targets, receiving the fourth set of reflected signals; and â¢ based on the fourth set of reflected signals, determining refined target information. 15. A method for radar detection, comprising: â¢ at a set of transmit elements of a radar array, transmitting a first plurality of multiplexed radar probe signals, comprising: 

 â¢ at a first transmit element of the set, based on a first encoding, transmitting a first encoded set of radar probe signals; and â¢ concurrent with transmitting the first encoded set, at a second transmit element of the set, based on a second encoding different from the first encoding, transmitting a second encoded set of radar probe signals; â¢ at the set of transmit elements, after transmitting the first plurality, transmitting a second plurality of multiplexed radar probe signals phase shifted from the first plurality based on a set of phase variances, wherein transmitting the second plurality comprises: â¢ at the first transmit element, based on the first encoding, transmitting a third encoded set of radar probe signals; and â¢ concurrent with transmitting the third encoded set, at the second transmit element, based on the second encoding, transmitting a fourth encoded set of radar probe signals; â¢ in response to generation of a first set of reflected signals via reflection of the first and second encoded sets by a set of targets in an environment surrounding the radar array: â¢ at a first receive element of the radar array, receiving a first portion of the first set of reflected signals; and â¢ at a second receive element of the radar array, receiving a second portion of the first set of reflected signals; â¢ in response to generation of a second set of reflected signals via reflection of the third and fourth encoded sets by the set of targets: â¢ at the first receive element, receiving a first portion of the second set of reflected signals; and â¢ at the second receive element, receiving a second portion of the second set of reflected signals; â¢ based on the first and second encodings: 

 â¢ demultiplexing the first portion of the first set of reflected signals to generate a first decoded set of signals and a second decoded set of signals; and â¢ demultiplexing the second portion of the first set of reflected signals to generate a third decoded set of signals and a fourth decoded set of signals; â¢ based on the first decoded set, determining target information associated with the first receive element; â¢ based on the third decoded set, determining target information associated with the second receive element; and â¢ based on the first and third decoded sets and on the first and second portions of the second set of reflected signals, determining target information associated with an interpolated virtual receive element location arranged between the first and second receive elements. 16. The method of Claim 15, further comprising: â¢ based on the second decoded set, determining target information associated with a first virtual receive element; and â¢ based on the fourth decoded set, determining target information associated with a second virtual receive element; wherein: â¢ the radar array defines a first displacement vector from the first transmit element to the second transmit element; â¢ the first virtual receive element is displaced from the first receive element by the first displacement vector; and â¢ the second virtual receive element is displaced from the second receive element by the first displacement vector. 17. The method of Claim 16, wherein: â¢ the first and second pluralities of multiplexed radar probe signals define a radar wavelength; 

 â¢ a length of the displacement vector is substantially greater than half the radar wavelength; and â¢ a distance between the first and second receive elements is substantially greater than half the radar wavelength. 18. The method of Claim 15, further comprising: â¢ in response to generation of the first set of reflected signals, at a third receive element of the radar array, receiving a third portion of the first set of reflected signals; and â¢ in response to generation of the second set of reflected signals, at the third receive element, receiving a third portion of the second set of reflected signals; wherein the first, second, and third receive elements define a substantially non-collinear arrangement. 19. The method of Claim 15, wherein: â¢ the first and second pluralities of multiplexed radar probe signals define a radar wavelength; and â¢ a length of the first displacement vector is substantially equal to lV2, wherein l represents the radar wavelength. 20. The method of Claim 19, wherein the radar array defines a second displacement vector from the first receive element to the second receive element, wherein an angle between the first and second displacement vectors is substantially equal to 45 0 . 21. The method of Claim 20, wherein: â¢ the set of transmit elements further comprises a third transmit element; â¢ the radar array defines a third displacement vector from the first receive element to the third receive element; â¢ a length of the third displacement vector is substantially greater than the length of the first displacement vector; â¢ the second and third displacement vectors are substantially orthogonal; and 

â¢ transmitting the second plurality of multiplexed radar probe signals further comprises, concurrent with transmitting the third and fourth encoded sets, at the third transmit element, transmitting a fifth encoded set of radar probe signals.
